task.prompt: Over long sessions with many multi-turn prompts, users notice the CLI becomes gradually slower and consumes more API quota than expected. Profiling shows the LLM-based loop check is firing more frequently than its configured interval (every 6-8 turns instead of every 10-15). The interval appears to drift across prompts even though each prompt resets the loop detector state. Investigate and fix the interval drift across prompt resets.

hint_levels.symptom_only: After several multi-turn prompts in a single session, LLM loop detection fires more frequently than configured, consuming extra API quota and slowing responses. The over-checking worsens with each new prompt.
hint_levels.file_hint: The bug is in packages/core/src/services/loopDetectionService.ts in the reset() method and updateCheckInterval() logic. The interval calculation uses floating-point arithmetic that can stabilize at a sub-optimal value across prompt resets.
hint_levels.function_hint: At lines 710-715, llmCheckInterval = Math.round(5 + 10 * (1 - confidence)). When confidence hovers just below threshold (e.g. 0.88), this stabilizes at 6-7 rather than ever reaching MAX_LLM_CHECK_INTERVAL (15). The reset() method resets turnsInCurrentPrompt but the accumulated interval drift from low-confidence patterns persists.

reasoning_chain: Read the symptom: LLM loop check fires too frequently across prompts, drifts with each session
Open loopDetectionService.ts and find the reset() method
Find updateCheckInterval() at lines 710-715 — interval formula using confidence
Trace: with confidence 0.88, interval = round(5 + 10*0.12) = 6, never reaches 15
Open client.ts to see when reset() is called between prompts
Open hooks/types.ts to understand prompt lifecycle events
Read test file to see what interval behavior is expected
Apply fix: ensure reset() fully resets llmCheckInterval to DEFAULT, not just turnsInCurrentPrompt
